skill.name: debugging-strategies
skill.description: Apply systematic debugging methods, profiling tools, and root cause analysis across codebases and technology stacks.
skill.summary: Use structured debugging methods to reproduce, isolate, diagnose, and verify software defects.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported dynamic imports, shell backticks, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and a URL, but context shows documentation examples. No executable files or prompt injection were found; low risk remains because the skill teaches command-line debugging workflows.

low_findings.title: Command Examples Require User Judgment
low_findings.description: The skill includes git bisect, debugger, profiling, and production debugging examples. These are legitimate instructions, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples, not executable skill code. The residual risk is user misuse of debugging commands in real environments.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The shell backtick and weak crypto hits are caused by markdown code fences, prose, or ordinary identifiers. No evidence found of actual shell execution or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are inside fenced documentation blocks or normal TypeScript examples. There is no runtime wrapper that executes this content.

low_findings.title: Local Profiling URL Is Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to localhost pprof documentation. No evidence found of external network access, data exfiltration, or remote callback behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is localhost and appears in a Go profiling example. It does not contact a third-party service or transmit data.

content.value_statement: Bugs often waste time when teams guess at causes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured reproduction, isolation, profiling, and verification.
actual_capabilities: Provides checklists for gathering debugging evidence.
Explains JavaScript, Python, and Go debugging tools.
Shows profiling approaches for performance and memory issues.
Covers git bisect and differential debugging methods.
limitations: Does not run diagnostics by itself.
Does not include the referenced support files.
Requires project-specific logs, errors, or reproduction steps.
Cannot guarantee a root cause without sufficient evidence.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Regression
use_cases.description: Use git history and controlled tests to narrow a newly introduced defect.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Production Behavior
use_cases.description: Collect logs, user reports, and metrics before planning a safe fix.

use_cases.title: Profile Slow Code
use_cases.description: Choose browser, Python, Node, or Go profiling tools to identify bottlenecks.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bug Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug this failing behavior. Ask for missing reproduction details, then suggest one test at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Working and Broken States
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these working and broken cases. Identify meaningful differences and rank likely causes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Profiling Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a profiling plan for this performance issue. Include tools, measurements, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Run Root Cause Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this incident evidence. Separate symptoms from root causes and propose tests that confirm each hypothesis.

output_examples.input: A checkout page fails only for Safari users after midnight.
output_examples.output: The response lists reproduction conditions, compares browser and time factors, and proposes timezone and client compatibility tests.

output_examples.input: An API became slow after a dependency upgrade.
output_examples.output: The response creates a profiling plan, checks recent changes, and suggests git bisect to identify the regression.

output_examples.input: Memory grows during repeated test runs.
output_examples.output: The response recommends baseline measurements, heap snapshots, repeated actions, and verification after the suspected leak is fixed.

best_practices: Start with a reliable reproduction before changing code.
Change one variable at a time and record results.
Verify the fix with tests that would have caught the bug.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize before measuring the bottleneck.
Do not debug production by making untested changes.
Do not remove logs or errors before preserving evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill execute debugging commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that users must review before running.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It includes examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go.

faq.question: Can it help with production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes evidence collection, safe investigation, staging validation, and feature flags.

faq.question: Does it replace observability tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps select and use logs, metrics, profilers, and debuggers.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes. It starts with reproduction, observation, hypothesis, experiment, and verification steps.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide error messages, stack traces, recent changes, environment details, and reproduction steps.
